title: oxytropis arctica subsp. arctica
intro: Oxytropis arctica subsp. arctica, also known as Arctic milkvetch, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the legume family and is native to the Arctic tundra regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Oxytropis arctica subsp. arctica include reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, making it a valuable resource for individuals living in harsh Arctic environments. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
